metadata.title: Diffusion / Fast-Follow (B3)
metadata.description: Even when AI writes most of the code, ideas still propagate. Papers leak, employees move, prompts get exfiltrated, and inference traces get reverse-engineered. The leader's relative advantage decays continuously.
metadata.author: Tracking AI Course

nodes.label: Model Capability
nodes.description: Aggregate quality of the leader's frontier model.

nodes.label: Visibility / Reverse Engineering
nodes.description: How much of a leader's breakthrough leaks via papers, employee mobility, prompts, and inference traces.

nodes.label: Competitor Adoption
nodes.description: How quickly rival labs implement and ship the leader's techniques.

nodes.label: Relative Advantage
nodes.description: Capability gap between the leader and the next-best lab.

edges.description: Visible capability invites reverse engineering and talent flow.
Fast-following competitors close the capability gap.
A larger relative advantage protects the leader's continued capability investment.

loops.label: B3: Diffusion / Fast-Follow
loops.description: Why every previous 'permanent moat' claim in software has eventually collapsed — the faster the leader moves, the more visible the breakthrough.
loops.behavior_pattern: Continuous decay of the leader's advantage through diffusion.
